senses.definition: to receive insults or bad treatment, especially without ever complaining

examples.x: He eats dirt every time he tries to show off his skills and fails miserably.
She eats dirt whenever she tries to impress others but ends up embarrassing herself instead.
I ate dirt when I stumbled and fell in front of everyone during the dance performance.
They ate dirt after their team lost the championship game by a landslide.
Tomorrow, he will eat dirt if he insists on challenging the experienced chess player.
They will eat dirt if they don't prepare properly and embarrass themselves during the presentation.

word_list.pronunciation: lˈʊk dˌaʊn wˈʌnz nˈoʊz æt ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to consider a person or thing unimportant and undeserving of respect

examples.x: She always looks down her nose at people who don't share her social status, treating them as if they were inferior.
The critic seemed to look down his nose at the work of the young, aspiring artist, dismissing it as amateurish.
Some employees felt that the manager looked down his nose at their suggestions and ideas, acting as if their input didn't matter.

senses.definition: (of jokes or remarks) close to the limits of what people can tolerate or find acceptable, particularly if it is offensive or sexually suggestive

examples.x: Her sense of humor is always near the knuckle, and she often makes jokes that push the boundaries of politeness.
The comedian's jokes were witty but sometimes near the knuckle, causing a few uncomfortable moments in the audience.
His comments about his co-workers were getting near the knuckle, making everyone in the office uncomfortable.
They walked a fine line between being provocative and near the knuckle with their advertising campaign.

senses.definition: to speak in a manner that is rude or sexually offensive

examples.x: The boss sure aired his lungs when we lost another customer!
You could've just asked Jane to hand you the file; there was no need to air your lungs.
He just went to the boss's office, aired his lungs and now he's complaining about losing his job!

senses.definition: to talk to a person in a way that is rude and shows no respect for them

examples.x: And he was not certain, not certain at all, what he'd do if the prisoner gave him any lip or tried to be smart.
When the teacher reprimanded him for not completing his assignment, he gave her some lip by arguing that the homework was too difficult.

senses.definition: resonating deeply with someone's personal experiences, feelings, or beliefs

examples.x: The movie's portrayal of war was close to home for many veterans in the audience.
His song about losing a loved one brought tears to my eyes - it was so close to home.
The story of the refugee's journey hit close to home for me because I have family members who have immigrated to this country.
The news about the natural disaster hitting that region hit close to home for the residents who had friends and family there.

senses.definition: to express contempt or disrespect to a person by showing one's naked backside to them

examples.x: He went to the window and hung a BA—just for the hell of it.

word_list.pronunciation: ɡˈɪv ɔːɹ flˈɪp ˌɛsbˈiː ðə fˈɪŋɡɚ

senses.definition: to raise one's middle finger and point it toward a person to show one is angry with them, hates, or disrespects them
to have an attitude that is inappropriate, rude, or too bold

examples.x: Jake has a nerve talking back to his mother like that!
She had a nerve to ask for a promotion after just a few months on the job.
He certainly has some nerve to critique her work when his own is far from perfect.
Despite the controversy, he still has a nerve to defend his actions.
The employee had a nerve to question the CEO's decision during the board meeting.

senses.definition: to talk about someone without giving them any respect

examples.x: He didn't appreciate it when his coworkers took his name in vain by making jokes about him behind his back.

senses.definition: (of a joke, story, remark, etc.) dealing with sensitive or controversial topics that can be uncomfortable or unsettling to hear

examples.x: The comedian's jokes about politics were a bit close to the bone for some audience members.
Her memoir openly discussed her abuse experience, which was harrowing and close to the bone.
The comedian's jokes were a little too close to the bone for some people, and they felt offended.
Her comments about my performance were close to the bone, but I knew I needed to hear the honest feedback.

senses.definition: a very private or offensive subject that should not be discussed

examples.x: Religion is a no-go area when you talk with her.
She made it clear that her private life was a no-go area.
In our friendship, discussing our past relationships has always been a no-go area.
At family gatherings, politics is often a no-go area to avoid heated arguments and tensions.
Bringing up the topic of personal finances can be a no-go area in some cultures, as it's considered impolite to inquire about money matters.

senses.definition: to laugh or shout insults at someone
